_readme.description: MediMind Lab Middleware — Analyzer Configuration Template
instructions: 1. Copy this file to 'analyzers.json' in the same folder
2. Fill in the actual COM port numbers from Windows Device Manager
3. Verify baud rate / parity / data bits match each analyzer's LIS settings
4. Set 'enabled: false' for any analyzer not yet connected
5. Fill in Medplum credentials in the 'medplum' section (or use .env file)
how_to_find_com_ports: Windows: Device Manager > Ports (COM & LPT) — each USB-to-serial adapter shows as 'COM#'
Tip: Plug in ONE adapter at a time and note which COM# appears — label the cable!

_setup_notes.cable: DB-9 straight-through serial cable (or USB-to-serial adapter)
_setup_notes.also_supports: Ethernet TCP — set connection to 'tcp' and add host/tcpPort if preferred
_setup_notes.vendor_doc: Sysmex XN-Series Host Interface Manual (PN: WA-2260)

_setup_notes.cable: DB-9 null-modem (crossed) cable — Roche requires null-modem!
_setup_notes.vendor_doc: Cobas c 111 Operator's Manual, Chapter 12: LIS Interface
_setup_notes.important: Cobas c111 uses NULL-MODEM cable (pins 2-3 crossed). Straight cable will NOT work.

_setup_notes.cable: DB-9 null-modem (crossed) cable — same as Cobas c111
_setup_notes.vendor_doc: Cobas e 411 Operator's Manual, Chapter 12: LIS Interface
_setup_notes.important: Same null-modem cable requirement as Cobas c111

_setup_notes.analyzer_menu: Host Communication Setup via Service mode
_setup_notes.important: Older machine — may need firmware check for ASTM compliance. Some versions use 7 data bits + even parity instead of 8-N-1.
_setup_notes.check_with_lab: Ask lab tech: does this machine currently use 7-E-1 or 8-N-1? Check the old Georgian LIS settings.

_setup_notes.cable: DB-9 serial cable (check manual for straight vs null-modem)
_setup_notes.vendor_doc: D-10 Hemoglobin Testing System Operator's Manual, Section 8: LIS Interface
_setup_notes.important: Bio-Rad uses LIS2-A which is ASTM E1394-compatible. Our ASTM parser handles it.

_setup_notes.vendor_doc: AIA-360 Operator's Manual, Chapter on Host Interface

_setup_notes.cable: Ethernet cable (RJ-45) — this is the one TCP analyzer
network_setup: 1. Assign the Maglumi a static IP on the lab network (e.g., 192.168.1.60)
2. Set the 'host' field above to that IP address
3. Ensure the lab PC and Maglumi are on the same subnet
4. Default TCP port is 5000 — change if needed in both analyzer and here
_setup_notes.also_supports: Serial RS-232 — change connection to 'serial' and add port/baudRate if preferred

_setup_notes.cable: DB-9 serial cable (check Mindray manual for pin assignment)
_setup_notes.vendor_doc: BC-3510 Operator's Manual, Chapter: LIS Communication
_setup_notes.important: This is the ONLY HL7v2 analyzer. Make sure 'Protocol' is set to 'HL7' in the analyzer menu, NOT 'ASTM'. Some Mindray models support both.

_setup_notes.connection_mode: TCP Server — the Mindray LabXpert software connects TO us on listenPort
_setup_notes.labxpert_config: LabXpert > Communication > Channel: TcpClient, Remote IP: <this PC's IP>, Remote Port: 5001
_setup_notes.default_protocol: HL7 v2.3.1 (ORU^R01 messages) via MLLP over TCP
_setup_notes.vendor_doc: Mindray BC-7600 LIS Interface Manual + LabXpert Configuration Guide
_setup_notes.important: This analyzer uses LabXpert middleware software — configure LabXpert's TcpClient to point at this middleware's IP:port.
_setup_notes.serial_fallback: If using RS-232 instead of LabXpert TCP, change connection to 'serial' and add port/baudRate/dataBits/parity/stopBits

_setup_notes.blocker: Siemens LIS3 is a proprietary protocol. We need the interface specification document (Siemens doc #10844061).
action_items: 1. Contact Siemens Healthineers support or your local Siemens rep
3. Alternative: Check if firmware supports HL7v2 output — some versions do (Settings > Data Management > LIS Protocol)
4. Alternative: Keep this analyzer on the old Georgian LIS temporarily

_setup_notes.vendor_doc: Combilyzer 13 User Manual, Appendix: RS-232 Data Output Format
_setup_notes.important: UNIDIRECTIONAL — this analyzer only SENDS results, it does NOT accept orders. Just connect and it pushes data automatically after each test.

api._note: REST API for MediMind EMR to query status. 0.0.0.0 allows connections from other machines on the network.

queue._note: Offline queue — results saved to SQLite when internet is down. Retries every 30 seconds, max 10 attempts.

logging._note: Log files kept for 30 days, max 50MB each. Set level to 'debug' for troubleshooting.
